evidence.title: UNDOF mandate and 1974 Israel-Syria disengagement framework excerpt
evidence.source_description: UN Disengagement Observer Force mandate page located by web search; direct curl returned CloudFront 403, so captured search/open text and corroborating UN Peacekeeping/Peacemaker snippets are preserved.
evidence.relevance: UN framework showing the Israel-Syria ceasefire/disengagement area is supervised under the 1974 agreement, supporting that the buffer zone is between Israel and Syria rather than ordinary Israeli territory.

evidence.title: Associated Press Dec. 17, 2024 Syria buffer zone report excerpt
evidence.source_description: Associated Press article located through web search; relevant text captured from search/open result for Dec. 17, 2024 report.
evidence.relevance: Independent credible reporting corroborating Netanyahu's statement, Israeli seizure of a buffer zone inside Syria, and Mount Hermon location inside Syria about 10 km from Israeli-held Golan Heights.

evidence.title: PMO Dec. 17, 2024 Mount Hermon statement, accessible mirror excerpt
evidence.source_description: Accessible GlobalSecurity.org mirror identifying source as Israel Prime Minister's Office, Events and Speeches, 37th Government, 17.12.2024; official gov.il source was also located at https://www.gov.il/en/pages/event-hermon171224 but direct curl was Cloudflare-blocked.
evidence.relevance: Official-source content for Netanyahu's Dec. 17, 2024 Mount Hermon visit and IDF continued deployment decision; supports control-intent element.

evidence.title: Times of Israel Dec. 9, 2024 IDF deployment in Syria excerpt
evidence.source_description: Times of Israel article by Emanuel Fabian, published 9 December 2024, opened and captured via web tool lines 104-184.
evidence.relevance: Credible reporting corroborating Israeli forces captured Syrian side of Mount Hermon/buffer zone, date, location, and Israeli official explanations including takeover language.

evidence.title: CRS Syria report excerpt on Israel's limited temporary UNDOF-zone measures
evidence.source_description: EveryCRSReport republication of Congressional Research Service report RL33487, Syria: Transition and U.S. Policy, updated February 26, 2026; relevant lines 389-396 captured via web open/find. CRS summarizes Israeli entry into UNDOF buffer zone and Israel's letter to the United Nations characterizing the deployment as limited and temporary.
evidence.relevance: Supports defendant's argument that official/CRS-described Israeli position framed the deployment as limited, temporary, and security-driven rather than an offensive intended to establish territorial control, while also preserving adverse context about entry into the UNDOF zone.
